summary: Matched the grstory data section by moving stage data previously expressed in asm/melee/gr/grstory.s into C declarations in src/melee/gr/grstory.c, then removing the asm object from obj_files.mk. The change introduced a static StageCallbacks array, a stage descriptor-like struct for lbl_803E274C, inline string literals for the OSReport call, and an unused static u32 array to preserve trailing data. It also corrected StageCallbacks in stage.h to model the fourth word as flags rather than another callback and changed lbl_801E36D8's return type from BOOL to s32.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/gr/grstory.s, which had contained the .data symbols lbl_803E26F0, lbl_803E2740, lbl_803E274C, format strings, file-name string, float constants, and null padding.
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/grstory.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk so grstory's data now comes from the C object.
Added static StageCallbacks lbl_803E26F0[4] in grstory.c, initialized with the same callback function pointer layout previously encoded as .4byte entries in assembly.

Added a C definition for lbl_803E274C containing flags, data pointers, callbacks, and trailing flag fields corresponding to the previous assembly data block, including the stage asset path "/GrSt.dat".
Changed func_801E30D8 to index callbacks as &lbl_803E26F0[arg0] instead of through the prior wrapper struct field lbl_803E26F0.callbacks[arg0].
Replaced OSReport use of string fields from the old lbl_803E26F0 wrapper with direct string literals: "%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n", "grstory.c", line 220, and arg0.
Changed lbl_801E36D8 return type from BOOL to s32 in both grstory.c and grstory.h.
Simplified StageCallbacks in stage.h: callback1 is now typed as s32 (*)(), and the former callback4 field is now modeled as a u32 flags field.
Removed the old extern wrapper declaration for lbl_803E26F0 from stage.h, including placeholder string and padding fields.
Added a static unused u32 array in grstory.c containing raw hex values matching the trailing data words from the removed assembly block.
smart_moves: Converted raw .4byte assembly data into typed C initializers while preserving symbol order and contents closely enough to drop the asm object.
Recognized that lbl_803E26F0 was better represented as a StageCallbacks array rather than a larger ad-hoc struct containing callbacks, padding, and embedded strings.
Modeled the last StageCallbacks word as flags, which explains the 0xC0000000 value in the fourth callback record better than treating it as a function pointer.
Kept hard-to-name trailing constants as a static unused u32 array, allowing the data section to match without prematurely assigning unsupported semantic names.
Used direct string literals for the OSReport format and source file name instead of preserving artificial fields in the old lbl_803E26F0 extern layout.
Updated the build list in the same PR as the C data migration, preventing duplicate or stale assembly data from continuing to link.
decomp_lessons: When matching stage data sections, first identify whether apparent mixed data is actually multiple adjacent objects: callback arrays, stage descriptor structs, strings, and unused constants may not belong in one extern wrapper.
Do not preserve padding-heavy temporary header structs once the data can be represented locally in the owning C file.
A word in a callback table may be flags or data, not necessarily another callback pointer; raw values like 0xC0000000 are a strong clue against function-pointer typing.
For data-section migration, it is acceptable to use raw u32 arrays for unexplained constants if that preserves matching and avoids inventing semantics.
Header changes should follow observed usage and table layout: this PR adjusted StageCallbacks globally instead of only forcing grstory-local casts.
Removing an asm file requires corresponding obj_files.mk cleanup so the new C definition owns the symbol/data contribution.
naming_conventions: Preserved decomp placeholder labels such as lbl_803E26F0, lbl_803E274C, lbl_801E30D0, and lbl_801E36D8 where no stronger semantic names were evidenced.
Used StageCallbacks for the callback-table type in stage.h and grstory.c.
Named the unexplained trailing data array simply unused, reflecting that no semantic meaning was established in the PR evidence.
Kept stage asset path literal "/GrSt.dat" in the stage descriptor initialization rather than introducing a separately named string symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a deleted .data assembly file with C static/global initializers that reproduce the same pointer table, strings, scalar words, and trailing constants.
Used a static array of StageCallbacks to match a contiguous table of callback entries formerly represented by .4byte labels and NULLs.
Preserved raw IEEE-754 float bit patterns as u32 hex words in an unused array rather than converting them to f32 literals, reducing risk of section or literal-pool mismatch.
Maintained direct raw constants such as 0x0000000A, 0x00000001, and 0xC0000000 in initializers where they were present in the assembly.
Changed the C object list to remove the assembly object once the data section was matched from C.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review according to the metadata, but no review body or inline feedback was included in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_803E274C or replace its anonymous struct with a shared stage descriptor type.
Check whether other stage files use the same StageCallbacks flags layout and can reuse the array-based representation.
Search for unused u32 arrays with float bit patterns in stage data to see if they were later typed or named.
Audit lbl_801E36D8 call sites to confirm s32 is the consistent return type across similar stage callbacks.
